revision.text:
[[ja:大日山35号墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.224417|N|135.220803|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} {{Kofun in Japan | name = Dainichiyama Kofun No. 35 | image = [[File:Dainichiyama Kofun Group-35, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
View of the Kofun
(Front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right, {{ill|tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}} on the far right) | part_of = {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11475228|lt=Iwasaki Chidzuka Kofun Group|1=Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} (Dainichiyama Area) | location = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama City|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Iwase & Inbe | coordinates = 34°13′27.90″N 135°13′14.89″E | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Mound length: 86 m | burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(Iwasaki type with stone shelves and beams) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | built = Early 6th century | Historic Site1 = Part of the Special Historic Site "Iwasaki Chidzuka Kofun Group" | notes = burial goods Historic Site1 as Important designation1 of Japan | map = Japan Wakayama | map_dot_label = Dainichiyama
No. 35 Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Dainichiyama Kofun No. 35''' (大日山35号墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Iwase and Inbe, {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama City|1=Wakayama (city)}}, {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}. It has a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} structure and is part of the {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11475228|lt=Iwasaki Chidzuka Kofun Group|1=Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}, which is Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Special Historic Site of Japan|ja|特別史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Historic Site of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 史跡|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Historic site|qid=Q1081138}}. The excavated burial goods are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. == Overview == Dainichiyama Kofun No. 35 is located at the summit of Mount Dainichiyama (elevation 141 meters), the highest peak in the western section of the Iwase mountain range within the Wakayama Plain near the mouth of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|en|Kinokawa River|qid=Q1042624|lt=Kino River|1=Kinokawa River}}. The kofun was constructed in the early 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. The tumulus is keyhole-shaped with its main axis oriented north-south, the front square portion facing south. It measures approximately 86 meters in length (including the plattype base, about 105 meters), making it one of the largest kofun in Wakayama Prefecture. The mound has a two-tiered structure built atop a shield-shaped plattype, with cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} and diverse {{ill|figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}} placed around it. The burial chamber is a double-sleeved {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the west, featuring early examples of the Iwase-type with stone shelves and beams. Numerous grave goods, including weapons, horse trappings, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, were excavated from the chamber. The site is open to the public as part of the Iwasaki Chidzuka Kofun Group, and the burial goods are displayed at the {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|en|Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore|qid=Q11417470|lt=Wakayama Prefectural Kii Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|1=Wakayama Prefecture Kii-fudoki-no-oka Museum of Archaeology and Folklore}}. == burial goods == The grave goods include: * **Ornaments:** Silver magatama, glass beads, jasper tubes * **Weapons:** Iron swords, arrowheads, armor fragments * **Horse trappings:** Shell inlaid decorations, stirrups, sa11le fittings * **Agricultural tools:** U-shaped plow blades * **Sue pottery:** Li11ed vessels, jars, and decorative items Many haniwa figurines were also found, depicting humans, animals (horses, birds), and objects like weapons. == Historic Site == * **Important designation1 (Japan):** burial goods from Dainichiyama Kofun No. 35, Historic Site1 on August 17, 2016. == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2070 Iwasaki Chidzuka Kofun Group] (Agency for Cultural Affairs of Japan) * [http://wakayamacity-bunkazai.jp/iseki/5000/ Dainichiyama Kofun No. 35] (Wakayama City Board of Education) {{translated page|ja|大日山35号墳|version=100306169|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大日山35号墳]] {{wikidata link|Q55529436}} [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Wakayama City]] [[Category:Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
